description:
Establish the Spring Boot 3 API project with all module boundaries, implement JWT-based authentication with refresh token rotation, RBAC via Spring Security @PreAuthorize, BCrypt password hashing, Redis-backed token blocklist, all Flyway database migrations, User CRUD, End Client CRUD, and healthcheck endpoints.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/auth>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/user>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/client>, <apps/api/src/main/resources/db/migration>
functionality: log in securely and have their role enforced across all API endpoints
benefit: every downstream module depends on identity resolution and RBAC being correct from day one

stories.summary: Bootstrap Spring Boot 3 API project with module structure and Flyway migrations
stories.description: Scaffold the Spring Boot 3 monorepo API application under <apps/api> with Java 21 virtual threads enabled. Create module packages: auth, user, client, process, session, question, feedback, job. Configure HikariCP, Spring Data JPA, and spring-boot-starter-validation. Author all Flyway migrations V1–V10 covering the full schema: users, clients, interview_processes, interview_sessions, status_history, questions, question_versions, session_questions, feedback, refresh_tokens.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Application starts with all modules wired
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Spring Boot application is started locally with a running Postgres instance
acceptanceCriteria.then: all module beans load without error and Flyway reports all migrations applied
spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true is active and thread pool is using virtual threads

acceptanceCriteria.given: Flyway has run all migrations against a clean Postgres instance
acceptanceCriteria.then: all tables, columns, constraints, and indexes match the database schema spec

tasks.summary: Scaffold Spring Boot 3 project under apps/api
tasks.description: Initialise the project with spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, spring-boot-starter-security, spring-boot-starter-validation, spring-boot-starter-actuator, flyway-core, and micrometer-registry-cloudwatch2. Set spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true.

Create packages for auth, user, client, process, session, question, feedback, and job modules under the base package. Each module contains controller, service, repository, domain, and dto sub-packages.
Write SQL migration files under <apps/api/src/main/resources/db/migration> covering all tables defined in the database schema doc. Include GIN index on questions.search_vector tsvector column.

stories.summary: Implement JWT authentication — login, access token issuance, and Spring Security filter chain
stories.description:
Implement the login endpoint (POST /auth/login) with BCrypt password verification. Issue a signed JWT access token (1hr TTL) containing the user ID and role claim. Configure the Spring Security filter chain to validate the JWT on all protected routes and extract the role for @PreAuthorize enforcement. Apply @PreAuthorize annotations to all service methods.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/auth/JwtFilter.java>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/auth/AuthService.java>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Successful login returns access and refresh tokens
acceptanceCriteria.given: a user exists with a known BCrypt-hashed password
acceptanceCriteria.when: POST /auth/login is called with valid credentials
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 response is returned containing a signed JWT access token and a refresh token

acceptanceCriteria.given: a login attempt is made with a wrong password
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 401 Unauthorized response is returned with no token

acceptanceCriteria.given: a protected endpoint is called without an Authorization header
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request reaches the Spring Security filter chain
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 401 Unauthorized response is returned before the controller is invoked

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user with role CANDIDATE calls a marketer-only endpoint

tasks.summary: Implement login endpoint and BCrypt password verification
tasks.description: Create POST /auth/login in AuthController. Verify credentials via UserDetailsService with BCryptPasswordEncoder. Return 401 on mismatch.
Generate a signed JWT with user ID, role, and 1hr expiry using jjwt or spring-security-oauth2-jose. Store signing key in environment variable.

tasks.summary: Configure Spring Security JWT filter chain
tasks.description: Implement OncePerRequestFilter to extract and validate the JWT on each request. Populate the SecurityContext with UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken.

tasks.summary: Apply @PreAuthorize annotations across all service methods
tasks.description: Add role guards on every service method using hasRole(...). Enable @EnableMethodSecurity on the security config class.

tasks.summary: Add unit tests for auth service and JWT filter
tasks.description: Cover: valid login, bad credentials, expired token, missing token, wrong role. Use Mockito for UserDetailsService.

stories.summary: Implement refresh token rotation and Redis-backed logout blocklist
stories.description:
On successful login, issue a refresh token (7d TTL) stored in the refresh_tokens table. Implement POST /auth/refresh to rotate the refresh token and issue a new access token. Implement POST /auth/logout to add the access token JTI to a Redis blocklist so it cannot be reused before expiry.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/auth/RefreshTokenService.java>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Refresh token issues a new access token and rotates refresh token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid refresh token is presented to POST /auth/refresh
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new access token and a new refresh token are returned; the old refresh token is invalidated

acceptanceCriteria.given: a refresh token has already been rotated
acceptanceCriteria.when: the old refresh token is presented again

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user has called POST /auth/logout with a valid access token
acceptanceCriteria.when: the same access token is used on any protected endpoint
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 401 Unauthorized response is returned because the JTI is in the Redis blocklist

tasks.summary: Implement refresh token persistence and POST /auth/refresh
tasks.description: Store hashed refresh token in refresh_tokens table with user ID and expiry. On refresh, validate, rotate, and issue new access token.

tasks.summary: Implement POST /auth/logout with Redis JTI blocklist
tasks.description: On logout, extract the JTI from the JWT and write it to Redis with a TTL matching the token's remaining validity. Update the JWT filter to check the blocklist.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for refresh and logout flows
tasks.description: Use Testcontainers (Postgres + Redis) to test full login → refresh → logout cycle including replay attack on old refresh token.

stories.summary: Implement User CRUD and End Client CRUD APIs
stories.description:
Implement User management endpoints (POST /users, GET /users, GET /users/:id, PATCH /users/:id) and End Client management endpoints (POST /clients, GET /clients, GET /clients/:id, PATCH /clients/:id). Apply Bean Validation on all request DTOs. Enforce role-based access — admin only for user write operations, admin/marketer for client reads.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/user>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/client>

acceptanceCriteria.given: an authenticated admin sends a valid POST /users request
acceptanceCriteria.then: the user is persisted and a 201 response with the created user is returned

acceptanceCriteria.given: a POST /users request is sent with a missing required field
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 400 Bad Request response is returned with field-level error details

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: End client list is accessible to admin and marketer
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 response with the paginated client list is returned

tasks.summary: Implement User CRUD service, repository, and controller
tasks.description: Create UserEntity, UserRepository (JpaRepository), UserService with @PreAuthorize guards, and UserController with Bean Validation on DTOs.

tasks.summary: Implement End Client CRUD service, repository, and controller
tasks.description: Create ClientEntity, ClientRepository, ClientService, and ClientController. Apply role guards: admin for writes, admin/marketer for reads.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for User and Client endpoints
tasks.description: Use Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers Postgres. Cover create, read, update, role enforcement, and validation error cases.

stories.summary: Configure healthcheck, Spring Actuator, and Micrometer baseline
stories.description:
Expose GET /health returning 200 for ALB target group healthchecks. Enable Spring Actuator endpoints (/actuator/health, /actuator/info, /actuator/metrics). Configure Micrometer with micrometer-registry-cloudwatch2 to begin publishing JVM and HTTP metrics to CloudWatch from day one.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/resources/application.yml>

acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 OK response is returned with a JSON body indicating the service is up

acceptanceCriteria.when: GET /actuator/metrics is called without an internal network header
acceptanceCriteria.then: the endpoint returns 401 or is restricted to internal access only

acceptanceCriteria.given: the application is running in a staging environment with AWS credentials
acceptanceCriteria.then: http.server.requests metrics appear in the configured CloudWatch namespace

tasks.description: Implement a public HealthController returning 200 with a minimal status payload. Exclude from Spring Security filter chain.

tasks.summary: Configure Spring Actuator and Micrometer CloudWatch registry
tasks.description: Enable actuator health, info, and metrics endpoints in application.yml. Configure micrometer-registry-cloudwatch2 with namespace and step interval. Restrict actuator to internal access.
